findings.evidence: grep hit: line 100 has `operator : ℝ → X →L[ℝ] X`, while lines 104-108 guard the laws/continuity with `0 ≤ s`, `0 ≤ t` / `nhdsWithin 0 (Set.Ici 0)`, and line 449 states growth only under `0 ≤ t`.
findings.fix: Index the semigroup by `ℝ≥0` (or otherwise quotient/extensionalize by equality on `Set.Ici 0`) and coerce to `ℝ` only in analytic lemmas such as the resolvent integral.
findings.issue: `StronglyContinuousSemigroup.operator : ℝ → X →L[ℝ] X` makes negative-time values part of the structure even though every law and result only constrains `t ≥ 0`; two semigroups equal on [0,∞) but different for negative `t` become definitionally different APIs.

summary: The main API stores irrelevant negative-time data, so the semigroup object is above the natural level for one-parameter C₀ semigroups on [0,∞).
